name: Wednesday Group Ride Bikes World
description:
Bikes WORLD Group Ride – Every Wednesday
Welcome to the weekly ride of the BIKES WORLD club (tag: BIKES), affiliated with BIKES France and reserved for non-French speakers.
Purpose: to ride together for about 1 hour, on a mostly flat and accessible route, without major difficulties.
📅 When?
Every Wednesday at 6:20 PM (UTC + 2), join us for Bonjour a toutes et tous. Hour of group riding on Zwift.
Each week, a different route to keep things fresh and fun!
⚡ Pace: average 1.5 to 1,8 W/kg. (Category D – beginners).
On climbs, the effort may be slightly higher, but the overall average will stay under 1,8 W/kg.
💬 “Riding alone is good… but riding with friends is even better!”
🎯 Goal: ride together, in a good mood, as a peloton — this is not a race.
Please stay close to the yellow beacon leader.
⏱ Drop-out fence: 10 seconds, to keep the group together.
📌 Sign up here → Zwift link
Join us!
➕ Add the (BIKES) tag to your Zwift username and join the BIKES WORLD club on Companion.
💬 Chat with us on Discord: ⁦https://discord.gg/a8wYvgw⁩
📱 Our Facebook: ⁦https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftBikes⁩

name: Vikings Valhalla Recovery Ride
description:
Welcome to the midweek Recovery Ride hosted by the mighty teams Vikings and Valhalla on Zwift! Join us as we embark on a journey through the virtual landscapes of Zwift, where camaraderie meets relaxation in a friendly and social atmosphere.
As the sun sets on Wednesday evening in Europe, saddle up and pedal alongside fellow riders who share a passion for both fitness and fun. This isn't your typical high-intensity workout—instead, it's a chance to unwind, recover, and rejuvenate those tired legs from previous rides or races.
Our pace is relaxed, our spirits high, and our chat is always filled with laughter and camaraderie. Whether you're a seasoned cyclist or just starting out, everyone is welcome to join in the fun. Share your favorite ride stories, exchange training tips, or simply enjoy the company as we pedal through the virtual roads of Zwift.
So grab your bike, hop on Zwift, and join us for an evening of leisurely pedaling, good vibes, and plenty of laughs. Let's roll together towards recovery, one pedal stroke at a time!
To find out more about our rides, please visit our Valhalla Ride and Race Facebook group https://www.facebook.com/groups/503388660165279/ or our website https://www.skaal.cc

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Glasgow Reverse
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Baltic Charge Race Series [12 of 12]
description:
Baltic Charge Racing League – Your Path to Peak Performance!

The league will consist of 12 stages, with only the best 8 results out of 12 counting towards the overall standings – so consistency, smart race choices, and tactical planning will be key to success!

Step up to the challenge in the Baltic Charge Racing League, where speed, endurance, and strategy come together in a thrilling test of skill.

Inspired by the rich cycling culture of the Baltic States, this series is designed to push riders to new heights with dynamic, fast-paced racing on Zwift’s most iconic routes. More than just a competition, these races are the perfect way to fine-tune your form, sharpen your tactics, and build race-ready fitness ahead of the classical cycling season outdoors.

Expect strategic breakaways, decisive sprints, and non-stop action as riders from around the world push each other to be better with every pedal stroke. Whether you're aiming for the podium or looking to refine your racing instincts, the Baltic Charge Racing League will keep you engaged, motivated, and race-ready.


Follow the action: https://www.facebook.com/zwift.lv/ and www.zwift.lv

A big thank you to our friends and sponsors: OTTENSTEN LV, 4CYCLISTS, ap print, SKODA, RAND & TUULBERG, ZZK, JETOIL, VOLTAS, WASH & DRIVE, NACIONALINE SPORTO AGENTURA, MADARIS, National Olympic Committee of Lithuania.

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO CC Race series.Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, C &D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

Please check out Facebook page evolution cycling for result and more information. 

#evo

name: Endurance Beat Pro
description:
Description:
This workout is designed to build your aerobic endurance while maintaining a steady and manageable effort. It consists of a long warm-up, steady state intervals at endurance pace, and a cool-down. Ideal for riders aiming to improve their long-distance capabilities without overreaching.

Structure:
• Warm-Up:
• 10 minutes starting easy and gradually increasing to 55% of FTP.
• Main Set:
• 40 minutes at 70–80% of FTP (Endurance Zone),
• 2:30/3:00 minutes of easy pedaling (55% FTP) between each 40-minute block.
• Cool-Down:
• 10 minutes gradually decreasing effort to % of FTP.

Key Points:
• Focus on maintaining a smooth, efficient pedal stroke.
• Keep your cadence steady between 85–95 rpm.
• Breathing should be controlled; you should be able to hold a conversation during the main intervals.
• Nutrition and hydration are important for sessions lasting over an hour.
Key Elements
Ideal for building aerobic endurance
Keeps attention high thanks to gentle variations
Great for indoor sessions focused on continuous worklink

spotify
https://open.spotify.com/playlist/4bYhqUicw1NFDS3NGmfoIv?si=-3iQtUVFShmoPAECG9tICw&pi=JJPhL0ssQBCrZ

name: ZLDR Starters Run
description:
Beat your first 15k and become a long distance runner

Have you ever wanted to be a long distance runner but intimidated? Don’t know where to start? How to get there? Are you coming back from injury? Have you felt that distance running was too far to run?

No matter how little you’ve run, no matter how “slow” you think you are, no matter what your circumstance. We would like to help you realize your dream of getting to that distant finish line.

ZLDR is a group of diverse runners, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners who will make sure that you are never left behind.

15k may seem too far for now but join our Starters Run and stick with us. We will help you build your distance and make it to 15k and beyond. Our starters run is the perfect place to ensure you never run alone. Our team of sweepers will make sure you have the support you need to get to the finish.

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn
ZLDR website for Challenge Months: https://www.zldr.club
Online shop: https://www.sweatydino.com

name: EVO CC Mini Vamos! Cat C+ [~2.8-3.2w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Cat C+ ride. These rides are geared towards riders that are looking for a higher Cat C+ ride where the group average will end up between 3- 3.2 W/kg, depending on riders weight. We will do ramps and intervals up to 4,5 W/kg for shorter period of time and up to 3,5 for 5-15 min. The group is based upon high social interaction so bring your best banter. Use the mobile app or a keyboard to communicate in-ride.
The event will be 60-90 minutes depending on the ride leader.

name: RAD RACE x SYN: Threshold Workout
description:
Threshold Session: 5x8min Threshold + 15min Sweet Spot

Goal: Improve your maximal aerobic capacity (threshold) and sustained performance in the submaximal range (Sweet Spot). This combo is the engine for your FTP development.
Intensity:

Threshold intervals: 7–9/10 – intense but controlled. Hard, but doable.
Sweet Spot: 6–7/10 – consistently demanding, but sustainable.

Body awareness: Threshold intervals are hard, but ride them with clean technique – quiet upper body, stable pedal stroke, steady breathing. In the Sweet Spot block focus on rhythm and efficient posture.
Fueling: 60–100g carbs/h – threshold and Sweet Spot tax your metabolism heavily. Train the gut!

Important: Attack the threshold intervals with full focus – then execute the Sweet Spot as a clean, sustained effort. Don't overpace the threshold work, or you'll lack power for the Sweet Spot.
After the session: Recovery shake or meal with protein & carbs within 30min. Hydrate well.

Warm-up & cool-down are part of the ride – can be shortened if you're riding indoors.

Build FTP, suffer with control – let's go!

eventSubgroups.name: RAD RACE x SYN: Threshold Workout (E)

name: Stage 4 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 4
Route: Sand and Sequoias
Date: April 6-12

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Sand and Sequoias, with loads of prizes still on deck!

name: EaStern Tour Coffee Ride
description: The Coffee Ride is designed as a relaxed and social midweek ride that brings the EaStern Tour community together. The focus is on consistency, fun, and conversation rather than competition.

name: Norwegian Watts Club Group Workout
description:
This is our Norwegian Watts Club Group Workout!

We’ll ride together, follow the plan step by step, and keep the watts flowing. Stay with the group, enjoy the draft (which we have not 😂) and let the guidance lead you — all you need to do is pedal, smile, and maybe sweat a little.



Workout changes every week, from hard to harder and from fast to faster! Join and have fun!

Norwegian Watts Club – Need more watts!

eventSubgroups.name: Norwegian Watts Club Group Workout (E)
eventSubgroups.description: Workout 3 in Zwift Camp: Inside Out is all about dialing in your cadence control. You'll tackle intervals at varying cadences, teaching your body to adapt to different terrain and riding styles. By improving pedaling efficiency and rhythm, this session builds endurance and versatility. Whether you're spinning fast or grinding steady, you'll leave this ride feeling smoother and more in control. Let's keep the wheels turning.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Reverse
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: 3R Steady State Endurance Ride
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: GXY RIDE & RACE [2.8-3.6 W/KG] – CAT B
description:
Welcome to GXY Ride & Race!
Looking for a Zwift event that combines friendly group riding with the thrill of competition? GXY offers exactly that!
– a unique format designed for riders who want both social connection and racing excitement.
What to expect:
This Wednesday's Cat B/C event unfolds in three perfectly balanced stages:
1. Social Warm-Up (1/3 distance) – ease in with Zone 2 riding while catching up with fellow riders. Share race
stories, upcoming event plans, or strategy ideas – all whilst getting your legs ready.
2. Building Intensity (middle section) – the pace gradually increases to Zone 3. Test yourself with optional
sprints if you're feeling strong, with quick regroups to keep the pack together.
3. Your Choice Finale – after a final regroup, the leader gives the green light and it's game on! Bring your
competitive spirit for the race finish, or stick with the leader's suggested pace for a challenging but controlled
conclusion.
Event details:
• Distance: 42-65km (26-40 miles) or time specific 60,75mins etc
• Elevation: varies from rolling terrain to challenging Valhalla-worthy climbs
• Courses: features exclusive "Event Only" routes – perfect for badge hunters!
Want to race from the start line? Feel free! This flexible format welcomes both social riders and racers alike.
Join GXY for great riding, team camaraderie, and memorable banter – where we ride as one, embrace creative
strategies, and always finish with smiles (despite the exhaustion)!
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed their goals.

A purely social ride that aims to cater for everyone, the ride is banded so everyone stays together no matter what power you can ride at.
So come and join Rhino Racing for a virtual roll, enjoy the company and have a fun Wednesday ride out. It's all about the ride!!
New riders and questions welcome.

name: Team Italy Hunting Ride
description:
Three categories will start a few minutes apart…(Category D, then C and B)
From 30 to 40 km of collaboration). Many km at a calibrated speed... all for the final goal: uniting 3 groups a few kilometers from the finish... and then unleashing hell for the last sprint..
Who will be able to collaborate better than the others? Who will be able to save energy for the finish line?
It's not a race, but an HUNTING RIDE... it's up to you to hunt or become easy prey...
#TEAMITALY #JERSEYUNLOCK

name: Dragons RT Raceday
description:
Welcome to the weekly Dragons RT Raceday, the midweek race designed for riders who want more than just another group ride.

Every Wednesday, we race a new route in a full-gas scratch race with all categories visible and a single start.

This event is the ideal transfer race between ZRL on Tuesday and TTT on Thursday, and a perfect opportunity for riders who missed ZRL or are not racing TTT but still want a competitive, fair, and meaningful race.

Power-ups available: Feather, Aero, Draft Boost.

To ensure fair racing, a smart trainer, power meter, and heart rate monitor are required.

No zPower riders and no TT bikes.

Final official results are published on ZwiftPower, divided into A, B, C, and D pace groups using Zwift category enforcement.

Riders with unrealistic performances may be excluded until validation is completed.

For the Dragons Racing Team, this race is also a platform to identify new talent. Strong performances, race intelligence, and commitment will be noticed.

Bring your legs.
Bring your head.
Bring your courage.

Work hard. Race fair. Have fun.

And maybe — just maybe — earn your place among the Dragons.

Let’s achieve greatness together.

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt12 BIKE
description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: Bikealicious Zone 2 Fat Burner Ride
description: This is a rubberband Zone 2 fat-burning ride. While the primary goal is to maintain a Zone 2 intensity, feel free to ride at any pace you prefer. We have riders of all levels; all are welcome. We support less experienced riders and draw inspiration from those with more strength and expertise. This is a social ride, so don't be afraid to jump in and chat.

name: Micro Mountain Massif Race 1 - s/b Rowe & King
description:
This event is designed so you can go "full throttle" on a climb for a very short period of time and to push yourself beyond your perceived limits, going shoulder to shoulder with fellow racers.

To win, you may require to employ tactics. Do you save energy on the flat section before going hard on the climb? Do you opt to go hard from the start? Only you can decide and the decision you make will impact your results.

If you are not interested in racing for the victory, treat this event as a test of your 5-minute power. Generate a consistently high power for 5 minutes and boost your ratings in Zwift Power. This race is unique and offers something for all.

Rules: 
Heart Rate monitor required and Zwift Power for official results, winner of each category.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: The HERD Bull Run
description:
Are you looking for a mid paced ride on a Wednesday evening?

Join the Herd as we tackle an assortment of routes, flat and hilly on Wednesday evenings 1800GMT 1900CET with a moderate pace of 1.9-2.2w/kg aiming for a average of 2.0w/kg for the full ride

Get practicing your moo'ing! Auditions for lead cow will take place during the ride.

Respect the leader's pace and ride with the yellow beacon. This is NOT A RACE! Stronger riders are encouraged to fall back and help sweep the riders struggling in the back.

WE DO RUN THE FENCE! The fence is in place to ensure that new group riders don't get caught up with the flyers and have a bad experience. Bear in mind that the fence will move due to changes in speed on the inclines. We ask that you always ride with the yellow beacon. Please pay attention to messages from the ride leader regarding fence intervals and when to regroup.

Join us on Discord during the ride to participate in audio conversation with one another. It will help the time pass by and give you a space to ask all the questions you have about Zwift and Cycling in general: https://discord.gg/Dr7ZtPV (Please use Push-To-Talk)
Check us out on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/zHerd/"

http://bit.ly/HerdDiscord

#herd

name: The GT Crew Weekly Ride
description:
Welcome to the GT crew weekly group ride.

We are the team who formerly lead the Geraint Thomas rides for several years when they were on Zwift.

Those rides are no more but Chris and the crew are keeping the community going …the GT crew!

Our crew are all highly experienced in leading a well organised ride with lots of chat and dad jokes last 10 mins.

We have one beacon at 2.5 wkg and the other at 2.0 so riders can fall back from the front or just take it easier from the start.

Welcoming back our regulars and new riders 

Book Wednesdays at 6pm uk for the best mid-paced social ride in town.

name: HISP- The Route of the Week
description:
A race with HISP plus some free XPs? Can’t get any better! Every week we’ll compete on the Route of the Week, with three time slots a day, all part of a monthly league (4 stages)
⚠️ IMPORTANT: to claim your XPs, make sure you’ve clicked on the Route of the Week banner on Zwift’s main page and joined the challenge previous to the Race.
The league’s overall standings will follow ZRS and will be available on ZwiftPower.


¿Una carrera con HISP más unos XPs Gratis? ¡Mejor imposible! Todas las semanas competiremos en la ruta de la semana, con tres horarios al día, en una liga mensual (4 etapas)
⚠️ IMPORTANTE: recuerda que para recibir los XP tienes que haber accedido al banner Ruta de la Semana en la página principal de Zwift para inscribirte al desafío antes de la carrera.
La clasificación general de la liga se regirá por ZRS y estará disponible en ZwiftPower.

name: NGNM Women Crush Wed - Training Ride
description:
Hello and welcome to No Gods No Masters® Zwift ride for women ⚡️⚡️.

Join NGNM and become a part of a large international community of women cyclists. Together, we foster a spirit of mutual empowerment & support, and improve our group riding skills.
NGNM offers more than just group rides and workouts for women on Zwift. We also organise multi-day cycling experiences for women in Italy and Europe, bridging the gap between virtual riding and real-life cycling!

See the link in the bio below.

For this Women Crush Weds group ride, we offer two main group speeds: 
🟡 Yellow Beacon: 2.5 w/kg (C category),
🔴 Red Beacon: 1.7-2 w/kg (D category)
Join the chat on our Discord voice channel during the ride: here's an invitation link https://discord.gg/wywQsUaRYX

Group Ride Rules:
n.1: NEVER RIDE ALONE. Try to stick to the group or pair with another rider (min. 2 riders)
n.2: Above all, remember to HAVE FUN! That's what it's all about.
Every ride is different; we change the course each week and alternate ride leaders.

Join our FB Zwift group and a vibrant community of women like yourself at this link: https://www.facebook.com/groups/NGNM.Zwift/

NGNM x WRRS kit UNLOCK with this ride.
Did you know there’s a REAL-LIFE version of this kit?
Check it at this link: https://nogodsnomasters.life/collections/ngnm-x-zwift

JOIN NGNM's ZWIFT CLUB for women - UNITED WE ARE via the Companion App!
—————
About No Gods No Masters® (NGNM) - Smart Cycling Apparel for Women

NGNM is a lifestyle cycling brand dedicated to empowering and uplifting women through cycling. We are a women-led and owned Italian brand proudly offering high-quality cycling apparel crafted by our country's best artisans and industry professionals. In addition to our apparel, we organize the United We Are events: multi-day cycling adventures for women who ride road and gravel in Italy and across Europe.

Where to find us:
https://nogodsnomasters.life/pages/united-we-are-events
IG: ngnm_cycling

FB NGNM - Zwift group:  https://www.facebook.com/groups/NGNM.Zwift/
STRAVA NGNM Club:  https://www.strava.com/clubs/494550

eventSubgroups.name: NGNM Women Crush Wed - Training Ride (D)

name: Workout 1 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 1: Foundations
This workout reinforces “calm control” with mostly steady work, then finishes by sharpening leg speed so you can stay smooth at any cadence.

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: PUMP: Loop Logic III — Wednesday (3 Laps)
description:
Same Distance. More Laps. Maximum Sass. ♥️🌶️

Welcome to Loop Logic, the weekday series where we prove that "going in circles" is actually a superpower. We’ve curated a week of routes that hit the 19–25km sweet spot with enough climbing to keep your glutes talking back to you.

The Logic is Simple:
We start with one big loop on Monday and add a lap every day until Friday. One lap is a warm-up, but five laps is a statement.

The PUMP Vibe:
Power in Numbers: No band here, ladies! This is your chance to find your rhythm, tuck into a friendly draft, and realize you’re much faster than you think.

Elevation is Our Love Language: Expect at least 100m of climb—just enough to feel the PUMP and earn that post-ride coffee.

Laughter > Watts: We ride hard, but we laugh harder. Whether you’re smashing a PR or just hanging onto a wheel for dear life, do it with a smile.

The Mission:
Show up for yourself, stay for the sisterhood, and let’s show exactly what women-powered logic looks like.

eventSubgroups.name: PUMP: Loop Logic III — Wednesday (3 Laps) (E)

many riders in the Zwift Community who wish to have a paced ride with friends
and like-minded members of the community. The ride has not been organized as a
workout ride but as a social gathering. The average pace will be between 1.8 and
2.5 watts/kg. All riders are encouraged to stick with the yellow beacon. We are
stronger when we work together. Stronger riders are welcome to assist slower
riders back to the pack if they fall off. This should be a fun, relaxed ride to
help you get you ready for your next big effort, whether it’s a workout, race,
or a climb.

name: PAOLO BARBERA THE STAB
description:
Please visit www.heycoach.it or contact me 

Coach Paolo Barbera is a Certified Triathlon and Ironman Coach and the founder of Active Kids, a sporting clinic to help children and adults in the prevention and treatment of obesity through healthy nutrition and tailored training programmes.

Paolo is an experienced triathlete and cyclist. In 2017 he opened an innovative endurance training centre in milan, Tri60, specialized in indoor cycling.

It has been proven that Indoor cycling plays a key role to build strength, resistance and improve overall fitness and technical skills.

Paolo’s workouts are designed to mix different training zones aiming to maximize results in a very short time. Athletes following his workouts, at the right intensity and at the right cadence, have achieved surprising results.

Paolo’s indoor cycling workouts have different lengths to meet every athlete needs.

In the last ten years Paolo has developed very successful training programs for triathletes and cyclists

IT IS NOT HOW YOU START BUT HOW YOU FINISH!

GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests to face every workout at the right difficulty level.

name: Paracycling Group Ride hosted by NCF
description:
Paracyclists from around the world are invited to join this group ride hosted by the Norwegian Cycling Federation.

This event is open to athletes of any ability including those who ride handcycles, tricycles, tandems or standard bicycles.

We will ride for an hour at an easy pace so you can connect with your fellow paracycling friends and competitors.

This ride is configured so that everyone stays together regardless of power output so athletes from all classes can ride with each other.

That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedalling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop. 

You are welcome to join us on discord for a chat. https://discord.gg/vjaxtxc
#ncf

name: BL13 RACE SERIES
description:
Welcome to the BL13 RACE SERIES!

In BL13, we enjoy hard, innovative and fun racing. Our customised races will hopefully provide ample opportunities for all of this, over short but challenging courses that will suit teams and riders of all abilities and across all categories.

Each week there will be two races that you can do either separately, or back-to-back.  The courses are custom length, mass starts, with custom power-ups.  
EACH RACE WILL NOW PROVIDE A MYSTERY POWER-UP FROM THE PEN!
Some of the courses will have ALL power-ups available!  So do your homework beforehand and consider the courses and power-up allocation details below:

Race 1  |  18 Mar  |  Yorkshire - Duchy Estate (8km)  |  Aero, Feather
Race 2  |  18 Mar  |  New York - Everything Bagel (8km)  |  Feather, Anvil
Race 3  |  25 Mar  |  New York - LaGuardia Loop Reverse (8km)  |  Aero, Ghost
Race 4  |  25 Mar  |  France - Hell of the North (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)
Race 5  |  1 Apr  |  Watopia - The Classic (8km)  |  Ghost, Draft
Race 6  |  1 Apr  |  Watopia - Hilltop Hustle (8km)  |  Aero, Anvil
Race 7  |  8 Apr  |  Crit City - Downtown Dolphin (8km)  |  Draft, Aero
Race 8  |  8 Apr  |  Crit City - Bell Lap (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)
Race 9  |  15 Apr  |  Scotland - Glasgow Crit Circuit (8km)  |  Draft, Burrito
Race 10  |  15 Apr  |  London - London Loop Reverse (8km)  |  Anvil, -
Race 11  |  22 Apr  |  Watopia - Volcano Circuit (8km)  |  Feather, Aero
Race 12  |  22 Apr  |  Makuri Islands - Bridges and Boardwalks (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)
Race 13  |  29 Apr  |  Makuri Islands - Castle Crit (8km)  |  Draft, Ghost
Race 14  |  29 Apr  |  France - Sacre Bleu (8km)  |  Steamroller, Draft
Race 15  |  6 May  |  Makuri Islands - Neokyo Crit Course (8km)  |  Ghost, Aero
Race 16  |  6 May  |  Makuri Islands - Island Outskirts (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)
Race 17  |  13 May  |  Innsbruck - Innsbruckring (8km)  |  Anvil, Draft
Race 18  |  13 May  |  Bologna - Time Trial Lap (8km)  |  -, -
Race 19  |  20 May  |  New York - Hudson Hustle (8km)  |  Feather, Draft
Race 20  |  20 May  |  France - Bon Voyage (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)

RACE RULES
In order to be included in the official ZwiftPower results, the following rules are applied:
Category Enforcement is enabled for all races
All riders must have a ZwiftPower account 
Riders exceeding ZADA limits will be excluded from the results until they can provide dual recorded fit files to the event organisers
Riders with unrealistic performances (e.g. based on Coggan power chart) may receive a DQ until they can provide suitable verification to event organisers
The event organisers may request .fit files from riders, and have final say in the results
Heart rate monitors are compulsory across all categories (riders without will be automatically DQed)
Riders will be unable to race without a power-measuring turbo trainer or power meter (i.e. no zPower riders)
Dual recording is encouraged for podiums in A category, so please dual record if you are able (organisers may request to see duals)
A-Cat series winners may be requested to provide two forms of verification: any recent weight video (following Zwift protocol), and a dual recording from one of the races

RESULTS
Final results can be found on ZwiftPower 
Here you can also find the points structure (which may be different from the previous series) and series league tables for those interested in doing the whole race series
Individual and team series league tables will be updated after the event

We are here to have fun; harassment and discrimination will not be tolerated and could lead to a permanent ban
Please respect the rules and other riders!

For more details and results please visit the BL13 Facebook group 
www.facebook.com/BL13ZwiftRacingTeam

name: DBR Wednesday Race
description:
Welcome to the DBR Wednesday Race.
All will start with 1 minute between RS categories. Expect each race to be highly competitive and have fun.
Categories
Each race will be using Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) for categorization.
Race up a category if your RS is at the top end of a category. Or if you simply desire an added challenge.
- A: 1000 =< 690
- B:690<520
- C:520<350
- D:350<180
- E:180<=0
Your Zwift Racing Score is determined by how much success you have in races.
Better against people with higher ZRS, then your ZRS may increase. If you place worse than people with lower ZRS, then your score might decrease. Read more about ZRS here - https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh
If your ZRS increases past a category boundary during the series, your points earned will not transfer to the new category.
RULES:
A smart trainer or power meter is required. zPower will be removed.
Heart rate monitors are compulsory for all categories.
No TT bikes unless it's a TT event.
You need to have a registered account with Zwiftpower.com and accept their terms and conditions to be in the results of the race.
Misc.
Race organizers may request riders in all categories to provide a dual recording or a matching RL effort (Strava) in order to verify a Zwift race effort. Failure to comply may result in disqualification from the event. Failure to provide a dual recording or RL effort on three requests may result in disqualification from future events until a dual recording with a matching effort is provided.
Race organizers may exclude a rider from the results due to poor sportsmanship. Examples may be bad language, sandbagging, or other behavior in a disruptive manner.

name: ZTBR - Training Stabilization TTT
description:
Zwift Teams Brazil [ZTBR] together with Assessoria Franklin Almeida, provides this training with a focus on providing better "Stabilization" in the performance of athletes seeking better performance in Team Time Trial (TTT) events.

This training aims to help the athlete tolerate and withstand an accumulation of substrates produced by the body in sub-maximal activities, bringing more stability and control in time-based tests.

Franklin Almeida Consulting was created in 2005, with the aim of evaluating the physiological profile, developing training to improve your physical capacity, health and maximum performance.
One of the pioneers in distance consulting in Brazil!

Note: We will have other phases of this training in addition to Stabilization, they are: (Training for FTP Gains and Terrain Variations).

===
Português:
Treino de Estabilização by Franklin Almeida

Descrição: O Zwift Teams Brazil [ZTBR] juntamente com a Assessoria Franklin Almeida, disponibiliza este treino com foco em proporcionar melhor "Estabilização" na performance de atletas que buscam um melhor desempenho nos eventos tipo Team Time Trial (TTT).

Esse treino tem o objetivo de ajudar o atleta a tolerar e suportar um acúmulo de sustratos produzidos pelo organismo em atividades sub máximas trazendo mais estabilidade e controle em provas de conta relógio.

A Consultoria Franklin Almeida foi criada em 2005, com o objetivo de avaliar o perfil fisiológico, desenvolver treinos para melhorar sua capacidade física, sua saúde e desempenho máximo.
Um dos pioneiros em consultoria a distância no Brasil!

Obs.: Teremos outras fases deste treinamento além da Estabilização, são elas: (Treino para Ganhos de FTP e Variações de terreno).

name: ZZRC Midweek Mountain Madness & Sub 60
description:
A very warm ZZRC welcome to our Midweek Mountain Madness group ride. We think Alpe du Zwift is the best climb in the game, so grab a bidon and jump on your bike. Let’s go conquer those 21 hairpin turns and have some FUN!

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.


The Yellow Beacon will pace a sub 60 minute Alpe ascent at approx. 3.3w/kg. This ride will have cyclists of all abilities, from those going up in under 50 minutes, to those just wanting to make it to the top for the first time. We aim to support you all. Find a group riding at your pace, encourage and push each other to new PRs, and enjoy the climb!

Join the ZZRC Club on the Companion App, follow us on Facebook, but, most of all, enjoy the ride.

name: PACK🐺 1.5 Supportive
description:
This PACK 1.5 beginner friendly event is aimed at Zwifters looking for a group ride experience at an average lap pace of around 1.3-1.5w/kg.

On the flat, the pace will be controlled to ensure the group draft effect is kept under tight control with a target of 1.5w/kg on any hills or upslopes. The Leader will text instructions as well as use discord voice chat as communication is the key to a good group ride. Sweepers will also be in operation to assist in helping the group stay together as this is a non-drop ride.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

name: Little Tour Ghost Team Stage 1
description:
🇫🇷 Version Française
👻 LITTLE TOUR GHOST TEAM

Format #LTGT – Course aux points

    Préparez vos jambes et votre stratégie ! Le Little Tour Ghost Team adopte un format basé sur l’accumulation de points. Chaque étape compte : les sprints intermédiaires et les KOM seront décisifs, et la régularité fera toute la différence.

Accumulez un maximum de points sur l’ensemble des courses pour viser la victoire finale !
📅 Concept et Horaires

    Quand ? Tous les mercredis à 19h25.

    Durée : 4 courses disputées sur 4 mercredis consécutifs.

    Classement Général : Basé sur les points cumulés (FAL + FTS + FIN).

📊 Barème de Points par Course

    🟢 FAL (First Across Line) :

    Points pour les 40 premiers au passage de la ligne (sprints/arches).

    40, 39, 38… jusqu’au 40e.

    ⚡ FTS (Fastest Through Segment) :

    Points pour les 5 meilleurs temps sur les segments.

    10, 8, 6, 4, 2 points.

    🏁 FIN (Classement Final) :

    Points à l’arrivée de la course.

    100, 95, 92, 90, 89… décroissant jusqu’à 1 point.

⚠️ Condition de Participation

Pour avoir une chance de remporter le titre du Little Tour Ghost Team, la participation aux 4 événements est obligatoire.
🔗 Inscriptions et Infos

    S’inscrire au prochain #LTGT :Lien Zwift

    Consulter le calendrier :Zwifteur.com

🇬🇧 English Version
👻 LITTLE TOUR GHOST TEAM

Format #LTGT – Points-Based Racing

    Prepare your legs and your strategy! The Little Tour Ghost Team adopts a format based on points accumulation. Every stage counts: intermediate sprints and KOMs will be decisive, and consistency will make all the difference.

Rack up as many points as possible across all races to aim for the final victory!
📅 Concept and Schedule

    When? Every Wednesday at 19:25.

    Duration: 4 races held over 4 consecutive Wednesdays.

    General Classification: Based on cumulative points (FAL + FTS + FIN).

📊 Points Scale per Race

    🟢 FAL (First Across Line):

    Points awarded from 1st to 40th place at intermediate arches.

    40, 39, 38… down to the 40th rider.

    ⚡ FTS (Fastest Through Segment):

    Points awarded for the top 5 fastest times on segments.

    10, 8, 6, 4, 2 points.

    🏁 FIN (Final Race Classification):

    Points awarded at the finish line.

    100, 95, 92, 90, 89… decreasing down to 1 point.

⚠️ Participation Requirement

To have a chance at winning the Little Tour Ghost Team title, participation in all 4 events is mandatory.
🔗 Registration & Info

    Join the next #LTGT:Zwift Link

    Check our calendar:Zwifteur.com

name: The WATT Squad After Parties
description:
Join The Watt Squad for a weekly after-party series with a social ride aiming at 2,5 W/kg on average, and finishing hard with a mini-race for the last 5 to 10 km depending on the course. Every week we ride on different routes with a mix of classic and custom finishes. Earn points by joining, and earn more by finishing higher at the finish line. Follow this special league from November 5th, 2025, to March 25th, 2026.

Earn points with a valid ZwiftPower profile by

Finishing 1st - 3rd: 4 points
Finishing 4th – 10th: 3 points
Finishing 11th – 20th: 2 points
Finishing: 1 point

We start the ride easy at 2.0 w/kg for the first few kilometers and get up to speed at 2.5 w/kg in the group for the social part. Riders at the back can count on the help of a sweeper and stronger riders in the group. Wait for the signal from the leader to start your fast finish. Results will be displayed when you cross the finish line. Please follow the leader’s instructions to make it fun for all riders!

Follow your results on ZwiftPower on our own League: The WATT Squad After Parties.

Join The Watt Squad in your Companion App and look for our other events as well. Interested in joining? Then reach out to us on https://www.facebook.com/groups/thewattsquad

name: ZZRC Happy Hour
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Happy Hour 2.4-2.8wkg. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and is one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift.

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 2.4 - 2.8 on the ride, with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints, but the average for the whole ride will be as advertised.

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over-paced.

In the event of falling off the back of the main group, you will need to pace well over the group pace to bridge the gap and catch.

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to the group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process.

Our crew is a caring bunch who dedicated a lot of hours to this club but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift.

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps, and Ride Buddies.

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances, our Sweeps will not be able to help you.

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, with minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to the group.

All our rides have a 30-minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON.

Double Draft is used on this ride

We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel
https://discord.gg/QdeS3PtXTX

TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app.

name: ZHCC RUBBERBAND CLUB
description:
Want a 100% guaranteed no drop ride? want a 100% guarantee you can ride at your own pace? want a 100% guarantee you WILL stay in the group,? want a 100% guarantee that the nasty fence will NOT kick you...........THEN LOOK NO FURTHER, this is the ride for you.
Try it - YOU MIGHT JUST LIKE IT!
Our ZHCC Rubber Band Club has the “rubber-band” activated which means that no matter what level you are, you will be able to keep up and the group WILL STAY TOGETHER. The rubber band allows you to ride at your own pace, slow or fast and you are guaranteed to stay in the group.
The ride speed will be slightly less than you would normally experience in a group ride due to the rubber band effect, but we feel that's a small price to pay to allow all level of riders to remain together throughout the ride.
In this supportive environment it’s a great way of completing a full 1 hour ride knowing you wont get dropped or get dragged along by those naughty fliers!
ZHCC do not race each other, we ride as one, have fun and enjoy a group ride.
The ZHCC gang look forward to riding with you.
STRAVA : https://www.strava.com/clubs/518792
ZHCC Facebook page - https://www.facebook.com/groups/1163022533747527/Opens a new window
DISCORD : https://www.discord.gg/ukSdEQd

name: GXY HIGH ORBIT [2.1-2.8 WKG] CAT C
description:
Welcome to GXY HIGH ORBIT the ride with a twin space shuttle workout theme!
A 60 minute journey where the yellow and red beacons (shuttles) fly both together and independently of each other
bringing a workout that includes blast offs, thrusts and power shifts whilst keeping all the riders in one or two
aerodynamic blobs. .
What is for certain, this ride has a completely different style and vibe and brings a fun atmosphere whilst we work
together,
Take a break from the norm with our Cat and Mouse style HIGH ORBIT ride, it's guaranteed to make you work as a
team whilst undertaking an achievable zonal workout.
GXY are a friendly inclusive and supportive club who strive to help others succeed. We are looking forward to riding
with you today.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally
via our Facebook link below:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

description: Supported by Dream Bike, streamed live to the Velo29 Facebook page with professional commentary every Wednesday. For every racer who takes part Dream Bikes will make support the Daisy Chain Charity.

name: Workout Wednesday Run
description:
Join the Zwift Runners for a group run workout that will be of benefit to all distance runners.

The speeds for each group are based on a threshold speed of A group 15 kph, B 13 kph, C 11 kph, D 9 kph. We will start the intervals at that speed but increase the speed of the intervals we get into the workout. We encourage runners to choose the group closest to the effort level you want to run at and to keep to the nominal speed for that group to maintain the group dynamic. However if these speeds are significantly too low or high for you then you can choose to run in the group that is closest and then adjust the target speed up to 10% up or down in the game or via the Companion app.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Running in general then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook group or zwift.com/run #gozwiftrun #wow

name: Crit Cade Steering Race
description:
Steering Required: This event is designed to maximize the fun of Zwift Steering! If you have a steering device, now's the time to master those apexes and gain an advantage. If you do not have a steering device, unfortunately you will not be able to join.

Get ready for an electrifying dash under the lights in the Crit Cade Steering Racing Experience! This isn't your average crit—it's 8 laps of pure, high-octane racing Downtown Dolphin circuit, but with a thrilling arcade twist!

Power Up Pandemonium: This race features Power Ups! Strategically deploy your Boost Power ups to cut through the field. Timing is everything! Each Boost power up will give you a 10kph speed increase for 2 seconds!

Speed Boosts Galore: Keep your eyes peeled for additional Speed Boosts scattered across the course to give you that extra injection of pace when you need it most. But watch out because these boosts can move around, so you’ll need to be on top of your steering game!

Steering Required: This event is designed to maximize the fun of Zwift Steering! If you have a steering device, it’s the time to master those apexes and gain an advantage.

These events are held within our Zwift Labs club. This feature is still in development and we'd love your feedback on your experience.

name: Stage 4 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 4
Route: Sand and Sequoias
Date: April 6-12

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Sand and Sequoias, with loads of prizes still on deck!

name: BL13 RACE SERIES
description:
Welcome to the BL13 RACE SERIES!

In BL13, we enjoy hard, innovative and fun racing. Our customised races will hopefully provide ample opportunities for all of this, over short but challenging courses that will suit teams and riders of all abilities and across all categories.

Each week there will be two races that you can do either separately, or back-to-back.  The courses are custom length, mass starts, with custom power-ups.  
EACH RACE WILL NOW PROVIDE A MYSTERY POWER-UP FROM THE PEN!
Some of the courses will have ALL power-ups available!  So do your homework beforehand and consider the courses and power-up allocation details below:

Race 1  |  18 Mar  |  Yorkshire - Duchy Estate (8km)  |  Aero, Feather
Race 2  |  18 Mar  |  New York - Everything Bagel (8km)  |  Feather, Anvil
Race 3  |  25 Mar  |  New York - LaGuardia Loop Reverse (8km)  |  Aero, Ghost
Race 4  |  25 Mar  |  France - Hell of the North (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)
Race 5  |  1 Apr  |  Watopia - The Classic (8km)  |  Ghost, Draft
Race 6  |  1 Apr  |  Watopia - Hilltop Hustle (8km)  |  Aero, Anvil
Race 7  |  8 Apr  |  Crit City - Downtown Dolphin (8km)  |  Draft, Aero
Race 8  |  8 Apr  |  Crit City - Bell Lap (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)
Race 9  |  15 Apr  |  Scotland - Glasgow Crit Circuit (8km)  |  Draft, Burrito
Race 10  |  15 Apr  |  London - London Loop Reverse (8km)  |  Anvil, -
Race 11  |  22 Apr  |  Watopia - Volcano Circuit (8km)  |  Feather, Aero
Race 12  |  22 Apr  |  Makuri Islands - Bridges and Boardwalks (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)
Race 13  |  29 Apr  |  Makuri Islands - Castle Crit (8km)  |  Draft, Ghost
Race 14  |  29 Apr  |  France - Sacre Bleu (8km)  |  Steamroller, Draft
Race 15  |  6 May  |  Makuri Islands - Neokyo Crit Course (8km)  |  Ghost, Aero
Race 16  |  6 May  |  Makuri Islands - Island Outskirts (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)
Race 17  |  13 May  |  Innsbruck - Innsbruckring (8km)  |  Anvil, Draft
Race 18  |  13 May  |  Bologna - Time Trial Lap (8km)  |  -, -
Race 19  |  20 May  |  New York - Hudson Hustle (8km)  |  Feather, Draft
Race 20  |  20 May  |  France - Bon Voyage (16km)  |  All powerups (except burrito)

RACE RULES
In order to be included in the official ZwiftPower results, the following rules are applied:
Category Enforcement is enabled for all races
All riders must have a ZwiftPower account 
Riders exceeding ZADA limits will be excluded from the results until they can provide dual recorded fit files to the event organisers
Riders with unrealistic performances (e.g. based on Coggan power chart) may receive a DQ until they can provide suitable verification to event organisers
The event organisers may request .fit files from riders, and have final say in the results
Heart rate monitors are compulsory across all categories (riders without will be automatically DQed)
Riders will be unable to race without a power-measuring turbo trainer or power meter (i.e. no zPower riders)
Dual recording is encouraged for podiums in A category, so please dual record if you are able (organisers may request to see duals)
A-Cat series winners may be requested to provide two forms of verification: any recent weight video (following Zwift protocol), and a dual recording from one of the races

RESULTS
Final results can be found on ZwiftPower 
Here you can also find the points structure (which may be different from the previous series) and series league tables for those interested in doing the whole race series
Individual and team series league tables will be updated after the event

We are here to have fun; harassment and discrimination will not be tolerated and could lead to a permanent ban
Please respect the rules and other riders!

For more details and results please visit the BL13 Facebook group 
www.facebook.com/BL13ZwiftRacingTeam

name: ZZRC Static Spinners Sub 2
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Static Spinners Sub 2. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 1.7-1.9 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be sub 2 .

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicated a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .


We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel for our sub 2
https://discord.gg/RJ3dhbjCEp


TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: VirtuSlo Wacky Wednesday Race
description:
Welcome to our VirtuSlo Wacky Wednesday Race. 
The Rules are simple: finish as fast as possible and have Fun!


PEN -> Zwift Racing Score
Pen A / 666 - 800
Pen B / 555 - 666
Pen C / 444 - 555
Pen D / 333 - 444
Pen E / 0 - 333
- You may increase your Zwift Racing Score up to 30-points over the PEN Range as a result of the race and your race will stand. If you breach PEN Range Limits by >30-points, then it is likely you didn’t have a maximal 5-minute effort in your 90-day history and you will be upgraded to the correct PEN.

RULES AND RACE Settings
- No Powerups
- No TT Bikes
- Participants must be registered on zwiftpower.com to be included in the results
- Smart trainer or Power Meter required.
- Heart Rate Monitor required.
- No Indoor Bikes without Controlled Resistance (Cadence=Power)
- Sticky Watts or unusual pedalling technique to get an unrealistic advantage will result in DQ

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS
- Zwift Activities must be saved PUBLIC for verification.
- Elite Level Performances above the limits stated below must be supported by DUAL POWER Recordings uploaded to Zwiftpower and with margin off error of less than 4%. Outside KOM efforts on Strava can be used for verification of Dual Power analysis.
*MEN: above 4.8W/kg or 350W for 20min, 5.5W/kg or 425W for 5min, 9W/kg for 1min.
**WOMEN: above 4.5W/kg or 280W for 20min, 5W/kg or 325w for 5min, 7.5W/kg for 1min.

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER?
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Zwiftpower results: questions, complaints, etc., please contact us https://virtuslo.cc/

#VirtuSlo #WackyWednesday

name: Micro Mountain Massif Race 3 - Powered by Muc-Off
description:
This event is designed so you can go "full throttle" on a climb for a very short period of time and to push yourself beyond your perceived limits, going shoulder to shoulder with fellow racers.

To win, you may require to employ tactics. Do you save energy on the flat section before going hard on the climb? Do you opt to go hard from the start? Only you can decide and the decision you make will impact your results.

If you are not interested in racing for the victory, treat this event as a test of your 5-minute power. Generate a consistently high power for 5 minutes and boost your ratings in Zwift Power. This race is unique and offers something for all.

Rules: 
Heart Rate monitor required and Zwift Power for official results, winner of each category.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Reverse
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

name: KLUB Wednesday - Kilometers. Lactate. Unity. Breakaways.
description:
🌎 ENGLISH DESCRIPTION // GERMAN BELOW

Kilometers. Lactate. Unity. Breakaways.
This is what defines the KLUB CYCLING COMMUNITY.

Join the KLUB in the Zwift Club section. 💟

A structured ride, combining endurance, optional racing and tactics in one format. With a leader in the ride, we try to maintain a pace of 2 to 3 Wkg of the peloton.

💬 Use the chat function to socialize the ride!

🔵 Kilometers
Every ride covers at least 40 km.

🔴 Lactate
All segments (sprint & KOM) and the final across the finish line are open to everyone who's up for it. Just like the town sign sprints out there. 😎 You know how it is, in german we call it #eseskalierteh 🔥

🟢 Unity
Fast times require teamwork. 💨 Find a group 💨 Work together 💨 Ride smarter, not alone

🟡 Breakaways
Finishing position matters. 🏁 Will you attack early or wait for the right moment?

🏆 ZWIFTPOWER COMPETITION

👥 Individual Rankings: Separate rankings for men & women based on:

🚀 Sprints (FTS)
⛰ KOMs (FTS)
🏁 Finish (FAL)

👉 In case of equal points, finish position decides

📊 POINTS SYSTEM

🚀 Sprints (FTS)
Top 20: 30, 29, 28, …
From place 20+: 10 points

⛰ KOMs (FTS)
Top 20: 60, 58, 56, …
From place 20+: 20 points

🏁 Finish (FAL)
Top 40: 50, 49, 48, …
From place 40+: 10 points

🌞 SERIES // KLUB SUMMER BREAKAWAY

🗓️ jeden Mittwoch von April bis September
🔗 https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=3252

🔥 Season-long competition
🔥 Additional ranking by Zwift Racing Score

📈 Event points:
Top 20: 25, 24, 23, …
From place 20+: 5 points

♻️ 8 drop results

👥 Team Ranking: Top 5 riders per team count towards the standings.

🎁 UNLOCK

Complete the ride to unlock the 👕 KLUB Kit in your Zwift Garage.

⚙️ REQUIREMENTS FOR ZWIFTPOWER

❤️ Heart rate monitor mandatory
⚡ Smart trainer mandatory

----------------

🇩🇪🇦🇹🇨🇭 INFORMATION ZUM RIDE

Kilometers. Lactate. Unity. Breakaways.
Das sind wir - die KLUB CYCLING COMMUNITY.

Tritt dem KLUB unter Clubs auf Zwift bei. 💟

Ein strukturierter Ride, der Ausdauer, optionales Racing und Taktik in einem Format vereint. Mit Lead im Ride versuchen wir mit dem Peloton eine Pace von 2 bis 3 Wkg zu rollen.

💬 Nutze die Chatfunktion für das maximale Socialride Erlebnis.

🔵 Kilometers
Jeder Ride umfasst mindestens 40 km.

🔴 Lactate
Alle Segmente (Sprints & KOMs) und das Finale über die Ziellinie sind offen für alle die, die Bock drauf haben. Ganz und gar wie die Ortsschildsprint draußen. 😎 Ihr kennt es: #eseskalierteh 🔥

🟢 Unity
Schnelle Zeiten erfordern Teamwork. 💨 Gruppe finden 💨 Zusammen arbeiten 💨 Clever rollen, nicht allein

🟡 Breakaways
Die Zielposition zählt. 🏁 Frühe Attacke oder auf den richtigen Moment warten?

🏆 ZWIFTPOWER ERGEBNISSE

👥 Einzelwertung: Getrennte Wertungen für Männer & Frauen, basierend auf:

🚀 Sprints (FTS)
⛰ KOMs (FTS)
🏁 Ziel (FAL)

👉 Bei Punktgleichheit entscheidet die Zielposition

📊 PUNKTESYSTEM

🚀 Sprints (FTS)
Top 20: 30, 29, 28, …
ab Platz 20: 10 Punkte

⛰ KOMs (FTS)
Top 20: 60, 58, 56, …
ab Platz 20: 20 Punkte

🏁 Ziel (FAL)
Top 40: 50, 49, 48, …
ab Platz 40: 10 Punkte

🌞 SERIE // KLUB SUMMER BREAKAWAY

🗓️ jeden Mittwoch von April bis September
🔗 https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=3252

🔥 Serienwertung über die gesamte Saison
🔥 Zusätzliche Wertung nach Zwift Racing Score

📈 Serienpunkte pro Event:
Top 20: 25, 24, 23, …
ab Platz 20: 5 Punkte

♻️ 8 Streichergebnisse

👥 Teamwertung: Die besten 5 Fahrer pro Team gehen in die Wertung ein.

🎁 FREISCHALTUNG

Schließe den Ride ab und schalte das 👕 KLUB Kit in deiner Zwift Garage frei.

⚙️ ZWIFTPOWER VORAUSSETZUNGEN

❤️ Herzfrequenzmesser Pflicht
⚡ Smarttrainer Pflicht

----------------

✅ JOIN our KLUB CYCLING COMMUNITY

🫶🏼 No Hate.
🫶🏼 No Ressentiment.

🌍 Who we are: We are you! We are everyone! Together we are your cross-team community, regardless of whether you already belong to a club or not. A community for all Zwifters, primarily from the DACH region, but open for the lovely world. No competition, no hate, no resentment. Instead, fun, motivation and healthy, sportingly fair ambition. We welcome all newcomers to the racing and event scene, help and share insights with each other. Go for it, we look forward to seeing you!

🇩🇪 🇦🇹 🇨🇭 Wer wir sind: Wir sind du! Wir sind alle! Wir sind zusammen deine teamübergreifende Community, egal ob du schon einem Club angehörst oder nicht. Eine Schnittstelle für alle Zwifterinnen und Zwifter, vornehmlich aus der DACH Region. Keine Konkurrenz, kein Hate, keine Ressentiments. Dafür Spaß, Motivation und durchaus auch gesunder, sportlich fairer Ehrgeiz. Wir begrüßen gern alle Einsteigerinnen und Einsteiger in die Renn- und Event-Szene, helfen und teilen gern untereinander Insights. Traut euch, wir freuen uns auf euch!

name: Inox Master Phenotypes Cup 💨 Sprint 1/3
description:
3 short races, flat-climb-TT, to discover your racing phenotype, with categories based on age (Cat. A=up to 29 years, B=30–39, C=40–49, D=50–59, E=60+):
are you more of a sprinter, a climber, or a time trialist?

The races take place 20 minutes apart, making this format ideal for high-intensity efforts with recovery in between, perfect both for racing and structured training.

Each race focuses on a different specialty:

Race 1 – Sprint: a flat course, ideal for pure sprinters
Race 2 – Climb: a hilly route, designed for climbers
Race 3 – Time Trial: an individual time trial, in which riders will use an advanced time trial bike, identical for all, provided automatically.

As mentioned above, this event uses categories based on age, not power:

Cat A (Elite) → up to 29 years old
Cat B (M30) → 30–39 years
Cat C (M40) → 40–49 years
Cat D (M50) → 50–59 years
Cat E (M60) → 60+ years

At the end of the series, an overall classification on ZwiftPower will identify the most complete rider, based on performance across all three race types.

Each race awards points toward the overall classification:
50 points for 1st place, 49 points for 2nd place, 48 points for 3rd place and so on

The final overall standings after all three races will be available in the following league:
https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=3096

CONTACT US
• Website: https://www.teaminox.it/
• Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/inoxteam/
• ZwiftPower: https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=16461
• Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cpr3rBGaZy

name: CSPT - "The Wednesday Gathering"
description:
Welcome to CSPT; a cycling and running club with a difference. It's a place where members can meet up & have fun without any pressure to perform.

Whether you're short of motivation or inspiration then CSPT is the place for you. Drawing on years of experience from all walks of life and athletic experience we can help to motivate you to achieve whatever your goal is.

With CSPT - "YOU CAN"

This event has no leader, no fence and no rules because that's not what we're about. Stay with the bunch, often we are banded, we'll help you just enjoy being part of the club.

Please join our club : CSPT for further details.

name: Good Time Riders - Pursuit Race
description:
Good Time Riders are a group of Zwifters from across the world who enjoy
riding, training and racing together. We race hard, encourage one another
to be their best, but enjoyment comes first. We have active teams at all
levels racing in Club Ladder, TTT, ZRL and DIRT series. We also run a
regular sub2 group ride for the Zwift community on Mondays, and a club
workout on Fridays. New members are always welcome, you can find us on the
Companion App, Facebook and Discord.

Enjoy the race!

name: FAAST CREW SOCIAL RIDE!
description:
COME JOIN THE CREW! https://taplink.cc/faastcrew
.
FAAST CREW GROUP RIDES! 🤘
Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday Zwift rides! Everyone is welcome to join… let’s have some fun and get them GAINZ!
.
TUESDAY FTP BUILDER! @ 18:45(gmt)
It’s time to put the work in… each week we will be aiming to push your limits with a workout aimed to boost those FTP numbers…
Come get involved and watch yourself get stronger each week.
.
WEDNESDAY SOCIAL RIDE! @ 18:45(gmt)
Social pace night (banded together)
The main aim of these rides will be to keep things easy and sociable… not only to allow for a wider range of capabilities to get involved… but if you’re looking to get stuck in on Thursdays ride, have yourself an active rest day and use this ride to get to know your riding buddies.
There will also be some “optional” watt bombs being dropped… get involved if you fancy some sprint training!
.
THURSDAY CHAIN GANG! @ 18:45(gmt)
Chain gang night (around 4w/kg)
Come get stuck in and let’s empty those tanks!
This will be a full gas effort… we work as a unit for the most part, let’s try keep things together and help keep the pack strong… and we will operate a sweeper up until the half way mark.
There will however be a sprint finish with results, so the last 5-10minutes it’s every man/woman for themselves. Get stuck in and try take the glory.
This will be a race format but the finish line is an imaginary one as the clock hits zero.
.
LATE JOIN is enabled, so you can join us up to 30 mins after the start time, with the event link.
.
Please help spread the word, invite your pals and be sure to tag us in anything you post… we like to see you suffering, so post on your story of you smashing those pedals and tag @faastcrew.
.
PLEASE JOIN OUR ZWIFT CLUB.
That’s where all the rides will be posted but if you happen to miss them make sure to follow our Instagram page and keep up to date with any upcoming events and announcements.
(All relevant links, click the tap link in bio)
.
We look forward to seeing you all there, come get involved and LET’S HAVE IT!
.
The FAAST CREW.

name: Team CLS Ride n Explore
description:
Come and join Team CLS for a mid-week ride. Clip in and relax as we ride at a steady C Cat pace with plenty of chat – Don't expect all weeks to be easy, some courses have a lot of climbing but we will support each other and get through them.

This is a chance to enjoy a friendly experienced midweek group ride with regulars who have no Big Egos. This one should definitely be in your weekly schedule as it adds valuable endurance km's at the same time that will improve your fitness.

PACE
After an easy roll out, we will settle into a relaxed pace of 2.5 on the flats and around 3.0 on the climbs, however short ramps may require some increased effort just like IRL. 

It is impossible to find the perfect pace to suit all on a ride like this, and as the leader can't slow repeatedly at the expense of others and a few dropped riders are inevitable, so we apologise in advance. One thing we can assure you we will not chase or talk about flyers.

Team CLS have some awesome leaders, male and female, whose job it is to keep you entertained throughout so they will always be throwing things into the mix on the ride.

You will probably be accompanied by members of our famous ‘ORANGE ARMY’, members of TEAM CLS CC who are dedicated to their vocation of helping others. If you have had a drop out or accidently hit a wrong key, type ‘HELP’ and they will come back and offer you a wheel back to the group.


TEAM CLS LINKS
Check out www.teamclscc.co.uk ...find out about our club, current events and leaders. 

Facebook Club Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclscc
Facebook Racing Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclsracing
Zwift Power Team - https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=1426

WEEKLY PROGRAM
Mon  18:50 UK - Team CLS Racing - Pace ‘n’ Race: Recon and Activation 
Tues 18:50 UK - This ‘n’ That - Endurance Training
Wed  18:50 UK - Ride ‘n’ Explore Club Rideout
Thur 18:50 UK - High ‘n’ Low - Interval Training
Sat  08:50 and 14:45 UK - Base ‘n’ Banter Endurance ride
Sun  18:50 UK - Chill 'n' Spin - Weekend Social


TEAM | CLS - Training | Racing | Community

name: Rhino Racing's Workout Wednesday
description:
Join our Rhino Workout Wednesday!

Each week we will have a Ride Leader (yellow beacon) leading a workout targeting an area of training that most zwift racers overlook, be it cadence, VO2, sweet-spot.
During peak racing series weeks we will try to lower intensity and focus on form (cadence etc) and then between big racing series we might dig into the harder stuff like VO2 Max workouts.

Come and join in the suffering every week and incorporate our group banded workout into your wider training plan!

Join us on Discord for the workout on our social channel - https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6 and if you have time, join our Banded Rhino Roll ride beforehand for a nice social paced warm-up - https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/rhinosocial

name: The Backpedal Race
description:
Welcome to the Backpedal race!

Join our zwift club and the Backpedal community on Discord!

Final results can be found on Zwiftpower.
Finishing this race will unlock the Backpedal kit!

name: RollCo: Rolling Tide - 10K
description:
🌊 ROLLING TIDE
Start small. Build big.
Welcome to Rolling Tide — RollCo’s season-long ritual race.

Every week, the distance grows by 1 km.
It begins as a short, explosive opener and steadily evolves into a full-distance endurance epic by year’s end.
This isn’t a one-off event — it’s a shared journey that rises with you.

🏁 Race Format
Rolling Tide is a categorised scratch race.
All riders start together, with categories keeping the racing fair and fast.
The only thing that changes… is the distance.

Week 1 → 1 km
Week 52 → 52 km

Early weeks reward raw power.
Later weeks demand patience, grit, and endurance.
The tide never stops rising.

Rolling Tide is about more than today’s finish line.
It’s about showing up — week after week.
Seeing the same names.
Measuring how far you’ve come as the distance stretches.

You’ll build rivalries.
You’ll track your progress.
You’ll remember the day it all began.

And by the end of the season, everyone who stayed will share the same truth:

“We rode the whole tide together.”

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

name: WTRL Duathlon Opt13 BIKE
description: WTRL's Duathlon League offers a unique and thrilling racing experience by combining biking on Zwift with the choice of running on Zwift or outside in real life. This makes it accessible to everyone and the ultimate test of racing versatility!

name: Cycling Weekly Club 10
description:
The evening ten is a staple of the British club scene as riders up and down the country will compete with their club mates midweek on a local course. Typically run following the post rush hour traffic the evening ten is as social as it is competitive as road riders, time trailer and club-men come together to compete, chase kudos and bragging rights.
 
About this race
Join staff members of Cycling Weekly, multiple time trial national champion Dr Hutch and a few special guests for a classic, ten mile time trial effort. Using a variety of courses on the Zwift platform the CW club ten will be a test of your power and your pacing. Results will feature on Zwift Power and in Cycling Weekly magazine each week alongside a profile of a competitor.
 
How to ride a TT
As the courses vary, so will your pacing. The flattest courses require a steady sustained effort. With the effort lasting anywhere between 20 and 30 minutes depending on your speed, you will need to know what power you can sustain for that amount of time, getting you across the line with little left in your legs. Finish too fresh and you haven’t pushed hard enough, but if you start slowing before you cross the line, you’ve gone out too hard.
 
When on the hillier courses pacing strategy becomes even more important. Time can be gained on rivals by pushing on the ascents and recovering on the descents, just don’t back off too much. But remember, if there’s a harder climb near the end, you’ll need to hold a little back to maintain your power output in the final push.
 
Your aim should be to ‘get it all out’. If you do this you’ll know you couldn’t have gone any faster. Come back each week and you’ll see an improvement, and be ranked in the series on Zwift Power.
 
How to join the event
Join via the Zwift companion app and be pedalling in a Zwift world well ahead of the event start time. You’ll be given a prompt to join the event in the bottom left of the screen. Do this and you’ll be taken to the start and placed on a conveyor belt where you can keep spinning your legs until you get to the front of the queue. When the time comes your avatar will speed up to 20 miles per hour for a rolling start.
 
About Dr Michael Hutchinson
Ten time winner of the national ten, six time winner of the national 25 and thirteen time winner of the national 50. Dr Hutch is also a best selling author having written about his attempt at the Hour Record and his thirst for going ever faster. He works as an aerodynamic consultant to Team Ireland and writes a weekly column in Cycling Weekly magazine
 
About Cycling Weekly
Established in 1891 Cycling Weekly is the oldest cycling magazine in the world and the only weekly publication. Covering international and domestic racing, the magazine also covers health, fitness and nutrition and reviews of the latest tech.

#cyclingweekly #club10
